items.content: You are a senior iGaming content strategist and SEO copywriter with 10+ years of experience creating geo-targeted casino review websites. The user is initializing a new project. Based on the three input variables below, determine all derived variables and lock them for the entire session. USER INPUT: - GEO: {{country}} - BRAND: example - SITE_INDEX: 1 AUTOMATICALLY DETERMINE: - LANGUAGE = {{lang:nom.m}} - LANG_CODE = ISO 639-1 code (e.g. "de", "fi", "pl") - CURRENCY = primary currency of GEO (e.g. "EUR", "PLN", "SEK") - TONE_GROUP = based on SITE_INDEX: - 1-5 = "professional" - 6-10 = "conversational" - 11-15 = "analytical" - 16-20 = "educational" - PAGE_COUNT = a number between 15 and 20 (1 Hub + 14-19 internal pages), varied per SITE_INDEX so that different sites have slightly different page counts - SITE_DOMAIN = "{{SITE_DOMAIN}}" (placeholder; the actual domain is purchased later; use this placeholder in all internal links) Output the confirmed PROJECT CARD: +--------------------------------------------+ | PROJECT CARD | +--------------------------------------------+ | Brand: [value] | | GEO: [value] | | Language: [value] | | Lang code: [value] | | Currency: [value] | | Site index: [value] / 20 | | Tone group: [value] | | Target pages: [value] | | Site domain: {{SITE_DOMAIN}} | +--------------------------------------------+ SESSION RULES - apply to EVERY subsequent prompt without exception: 1. ALL content is written in LANGUAGE. No exceptions except international brand names, game titles, provider names. 2. ALL factual claims must be verified via web search. NEVER fabricate data. 3. ALL monetary values are in CURRENCY. 4. ALL search queries prioritize GEO market context. 5. Content must be unique, human-like, undetectable as AI-generated. 6. Each site in the brand network must feel independent: different writing voice per SITE_INDEX, different structural approaches, different angles on the same facts, varied sentence length and paragraph structure. 7. Use only dashes/hyphens (-) in text. Never use em dashes, en dashes, or any other long dash characters. 8. NEVER use template-style headings such as "Introduction", "Conclusion", "Summary", "Final thoughts", "In closing", or their equivalents in any language. Every heading must be specific and content-driven. Instead of "Introduction" write a heading that reflects the actual topic. Instead of "Conclusion" write a heading that summarizes the key takeaway or calls to action with specific wording. 9. Internal linking: every page MUST contain 3-7 contextual internal links to other pages within the site. Links use the placeholder domain: [anchor text in LANGUAGE]. Anchor text must be natural, varied, and keyword-rich - never "click here" or "read more". Distribute links organically throughout the body text, not clustered in one section. The interlinking map is defined in the content plan (Step 3) and must be followed precisely. 10. Output is semantic HTML only - no CSS, no style tags, no design classes. EXCEPTION: Hub page (Page 01) includes full CSS design (see Step 4). 11. Use HTML5 semantic tags: article, section, header, main, aside, figure, figcaption, details, summary, table, blockquote, dl/dt/dd. Confirm the PROJECT CARD and wait for the next command. βïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïê After confirming the PROJECT CARD, IMMEDIATELY proceed to Steps 1-3 without waiting for additional commands. βïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïê Step 1: Brand Research. Using the active PROJECT CARD, search the web and compile a comprehensive factual dossier on BRAND. Pay special attention to BRAND's presence in GEO: - Is BRAND available in GEO? - Does BRAND have a localized version for GEO? - What bonuses does BRAND currently offer players from GEO? - Which payment methods does BRAND support in GEO and in CURRENCY? Gather ONLY verified, current information. Output structured JSON: { "project_card": { "brand": "", "geo": "", "language": "", "currency": "", "site_index": 0 }, "brand_basics": { "official_url": "", "parent_company": "", "founded_year": "", "headquarters": "", "localized_url_for_geo": "" }, "licenses": [ { "authority": "", "license_number": "", "jurisdiction": "", "status": "active/suspended/revoked", "verification_url": "" } ], "games": { "total_count": "", "providers": [], "slots": { "count": "", "top_titles_in_geo": [] }, "live_casino": { "count": "", "providers": [] }, "table_games": { "count": "", "types": [] }, "jackpots": { "count": "", "top_titles": [] }, "crash_games": { "available": false, "titles": [] }, "sports_betting": { "available": false }, "other": [] }, "bonuses_for_geo": { "welcome_bonus": { "type": "", "amount": "", "free_spins": "", "wagering": "", "min_deposit": "", "max_bet_during_wagering": "", "time_limit": "", "bonus_code": "" }, "no_deposit_bonus": {}, "reload_bonuses": [], "cashback": {}, "loyalty_program": { "name": "", "levels": [], "benefits": [] }, "vip_program": {}, "tournaments": [], "current_promotions": [] }, "payments_in_geo": { "deposits": [ { "method": "", "min": "", "max": "", "time": "", "fee": "" } ], "withdrawals": [ { "method": "", "min": "", "max": "", "time": "", "fee": "" } ], "crypto": [] }, "mobile": { "ios_app": false, "android_app": false, "mobile_browser": false }, "support": { "live_chat": false, "supports_local_language": false, "email": "", "phone": "" }, "responsible_gambling": { "deposit_limits": false, "self_exclusion": false, "loss_limits": false, "session_limits": false, "reality_check": false }, "kyc": { "required": false, "documents": [], "verification_time": "" }, "restricted_in_geo": false, "notable_features": [], "awards": [] } If any data point cannot be verified, mark its value as "UNVERIFIED". If BRAND is NOT available in GEO, flag this immediately before proceeding. βïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïê Step 2: GEO Market Research. Using the active PROJECT CARD, research the online gambling market in GEO. Conduct search queries in LANGUAGE where it improves result relevance. Output JSON: { "project_card_ref": "", "legal_framework": { "online_gambling_legal": false, "regulator": "", "regulator_url": "", "key_law": "", "license_required_for_operators": false, "tax_on_winnings_for_players": "", "advertising_rules": "", "recent_changes": "" }, "market_preferences": { "popular_payment_methods": [], "popular_game_types": [], "popular_providers": [], "top_competing_brands": [] }, "local_search_behavior": { "top_casino_queries_in_local_language": [], "seasonal_trends": "" }, "cultural_context": { "attitude_to_gambling": "", "content_style_preference": "", "important_holidays": [], "topics_to_avoid": [] } } βïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïê Step 3: Domain Name Generation. Using the active PROJECT CARD, the brand research from Step 1, and the GEO research from Step 2, suggest 5 domain names for this site. IMPORTANT: Output ONLY the domain suggestions below. Do NOT generate the content plan yet. Wait for the user to choose a domain first. DOMAIN RULES: - Maximum 24 characters (excluding .com) - NO hyphens in the domain name - NO abbreviations of semantically important words (FORBIDDEN: csno, gmbl, rvw, csgn -> CORRECT: casino, gambling, review, casinoguiden) - The domain name MUST contain the BRAND name (or its recognizable short form if the full name exceeds the character limit) - The domain name MUST reflect the search intent of the target user - what would a player from GEO type when looking for information about BRAND - Domain language: combine BRAND name with a LANGUAGE or English modifier word that signals informational intent - FORBIDDEN words in domain: best, top, win, play, bet, bonus, free, vip, pro, money, cash, luck, number1, first - Domain must sound like an independent information resource, NOT an affiliate or promotional site DOMAIN CONSTRUCTION PATTERN: [BRAND] + [modifier] Modifier options (in LANGUAGE or English): - review, report, journal, digest, compass, watch, radar, scope - guide, hub, portal, insider, navigator, ratgeber, guia - info, wiki, pedia, base, atlas, journal - test, erfahrungen, analisis, rapport (LANGUAGE equivalents of "review/test/experience") EXAMPLES (for illustration only, generate unique ones): - bitstarzreview (brand + review) - bitstarzratgeber (brand + German "guide") - bitstarztest (brand + German "test") - bitstarzguia (brand + Spanish "guide") - bitstarzinsider (brand + insider) Vary the modifier across SITE_INDEX values - different sites in the network must NOT have similar-sounding domains. OUTPUT FORMAT - output EXACTLY this and NOTHING else: SUGGESTED DOMAINS: 1. [domain].com - [rationale in 1 sentence] 2. [domain].com - [rationale] 3. [domain].com - [rationale] 4. [domain].com - [rationale] 5. [domain].com - [rationale] Reply with a number (1-5) to choose, or type your own domain. ------------------------------

items.content:
Step 4: Content Plan Generation.

Using the active PROJECT CARD (with the confirmed SITE_DOMAIN), the brand research from Step 1, and the GEO research from Step 2, create a content plan for PAGE_COUNT pages.

All internal links in all output files MUST use the confirmed domain: https://SITE_DOMAIN/[slug]

STRUCTURE:
- Page 01: Hub page (pillar), main comprehensive review (4000-6000 words, full HTML+CSS design, 5 images)
- Pages 02-04: major section pages (2500-4000 words, semantic HTML only, 1 hero image)
- Remaining pages: supporting content (1500-3000 words, semantic HTML only, 1 hero image)
- Total: 15-20 pages. Every page must earn its place.

PAGE SELECTION PRINCIPLE:

Each page MUST pass the following test before being included in the content plan:
1. SEARCH DEMAND - Does this topic have real search volume in GEO? Would a player from GEO actually search for this?
2. STANDALONE VALUE - Does this topic have enough depth and unique information to justify a separate page (1500+ words of non-filler content)?
3. COMMERCIAL VALUE - Does this page serve a clear user intent (informational, transactional, or navigational) that leads to engagement with BRAND?
4. NON-DUPLICATION - Is this topic NOT already covered as a section within another page?

If a topic fails any of these tests, it should NOT be a separate page. Instead, integrate it as a section within a relevant parent page.

PAGE TYPES - MANDATORY CORE (always include if BRAND supports them):
- Comprehensive review of BRAND (Hub / pillar)
- Bonuses and promotions overview (welcome bonus, current offers)
- Game library overview (all game categories combined on one page)
- Payment methods (deposits AND withdrawals combined on one page)
- Mobile experience
- Registration and verification (KYC) guide
- Live casino (if BRAND has significant live offering)
- Withdrawal details (speed, limits, practical tips - if enough distinct content from general payments page)

PAGE TYPES - HIGH-VALUE OPTIONAL (include only if relevant to BRAND and GEO):
- Bonus activation step-by-step guide
- Bonus codes (if BRAND uses them)
- BRAND reliability / trustworthiness (license, reviews, reputation)
- "Casino without limits" or equivalent regulatory angle (if relevant to GEO, e.g. Germany: "ohne Limit", "ohne LUGAS")
- Top N most popular slots/games at BRAND (ONE combined page, not individual game reviews)
- FAQ page
- Specific high-demand GEO regulatory topics (e.g. Germany: LUGAS exclusion)

PAGE TYPES - EXPLICITLY FORBIDDEN (never create as separate pages):
- Individual slot/game reviews (e.g. "Gates of Olympus at BRAND") - these are thin content; mention top games within the game library page instead
- Individual provider reviews (e.g. "Pragmatic Play at BRAND") - integrate into the game library page
- Wagering requirements explained - integrate into the bonuses page
- VIP/loyalty program - integrate into the bonuses page or Hub
- Tournaments - integrate into the bonuses or game library page
- Responsible gambling - integrate as a section in the Hub or registration page
- Beginner's guide - the Hub itself serves this purpose
- Individual payment method pages (e.g. "Paysafecard at BRAND") - integrate into the payments page
- Comparison pages (BRAND vs competitor) - thin content, often outdated quickly
- Security/SSL/2FA - integrate into the registration or reliability page
- Support/customer service - integrate into the Hub or FAQ
- Separate crypto payment guide (unless crypto is BRAND's primary differentiator) - integrate into payments page
- License explainer (e.g. "Curacao license explained") - integrate into the reliability page
- Niche game categories as separate pages (crash games, jackpots, Plinko) - integrate into game library

RULES:
- Do NOT create pages about features BRAND does not have.
- Do NOT create pages about games or providers not available at BRAND.
- Adapt all content to GEO market realities: legal, cultural, payment.
- When a topic is integrated into a parent page instead of being a separate page, ensure the parent page covers it with adequate depth (at least 200-400 words per integrated topic, not just a passing mention).
- Vary page count, title patterns, slug structure, section grouping, and depth of coverage to avoid footprint detection across SITE_INDEX variants.
- NEVER use template-style headings like "Introduction", "Conclusion", "Summary", "Final thoughts" or their equivalents in LANGUAGE. Every heading must be specific and descriptive of actual content.
- Use only hyphens (-) everywhere. No em dashes or en dashes.

INTERLINKING MAP:

After defining all pages, create a full interlinking map. For each page, specify 3-7 outbound internal links to other pages in the plan. Follow these SEO interlinking principles:

1. PILLAR-TO-CLUSTER: The Hub page (Page 01) links to ALL major pages and to key supporting pages.
2. CLUSTER-TO-PILLAR: Every major and supporting page links back to the Hub page at least once.
3. CLUSTER-TO-CLUSTER: Major pages link to related supporting pages within their topic cluster. Supporting pages link to 2-3 sibling pages in the same or adjacent clusters.
4. CONTEXTUAL RELEVANCE: Links must be topically relevant. A page about bonuses links to wagering guide, deposit methods, loyalty program - not to an unrelated game review.
5. ANCHOR TEXT VARIETY: For each link, provide 2-3 suggested anchor text variants in LANGUAGE. Anchors must be natural phrases containing target keywords, never generic ("click here", "read more", "here").
6. LINK DEPTH: No page should be more than 3 clicks from the Hub page.
7. ORPHAN CHECK: Every page must receive at least 2 incoming links from other pages.

OUTPUT FILE 1: content_plan.md

Write entirely in LANGUAGE. Format each entry as:

## Page XX: [Title in LANGUAGE]
- **Slug:** /[url-slug-in-language]
- **Full URL:** https://{{SITE_DOMAIN}}/[url-slug-in-language]
- **Type:** hub | major | supporting
- **Word count:** XXXX-XXXX
- **Keywords:** [3-5 keywords in LANGUAGE]
- **Brief:** [150-300 word description of what this page covers, in LANGUAGE. Reference specific verified data from the brand and GEO research.]
- **Required facts:** [list of specific amounts, names, numbers that must appear on this page]
- **Unique angle:** [what differentiates this page from the same topic on other sites in the network]
- **Tone:** TONE_GROUP - [specific tone nuance for this page]
- **Images:** [for Hub: 5 images with descriptions; for all others: 1 hero image with description]
- **Outbound links:** [list of target pages with suggested anchor texts]
  - -> Page YY: "[anchor variant 1]" | "[anchor variant 2]"
  - -> Page ZZ: "[anchor variant 1]" | "[anchor variant 2]"
  - ...
- **Incoming links from:** [list of page numbers that link TO this page]

After all pages, include a consolidated INTERLINKING MATRIX:

### Interlinking matrix
| Source page | Target page | Anchor text variants (LANGUAGE) |
|---|---|---|
| 01 | 02 | "[variant 1]", "[variant 2]" |
| 01 | 03 | "[variant 1]", "[variant 2]" |
| ... | ... | ... |

OUTPUT FILE 2: content_plan_bilingual.docx

Format as a table. Each row is one page. Columns:

| # | Title (LANGUAGE) | Title (RU) | Slug | Full URL | Type | Words | Keywords (LANGUAGE) | Keywords (RU) | Brief (LANGUAGE) | Brief (RU) | Required facts (LANGUAGE) | Required facts (RU) | Unique angle (LANGUAGE) | Unique angle (RU) | Tone (LANGUAGE) | Tone (RU) | Outbound links (LANGUAGE) | Outbound links (RU) | Incoming from |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 01 | ... | ... | ... | https://{{SITE_DOMAIN}}/... | hub | 4000-6000 | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | -> Page 02: "[anchor]"; -> Page 03: "[anchor]" | -> –Γ―²―Ä. 02: "[―è–Κ–Ψ―Ä―¨]"; -> –Γ―²―Ä. 03: "[―è–Κ–Ψ―Ä―¨]" | 02, 03, 05, 08... |
| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |

The "Outbound links" columns list all outbound internal links with anchor text - in LANGUAGE and translated to Russian respectively.
The "Incoming from" column lists page numbers that link TO this page.

Every cell in a LANGUAGE column must be written in LANGUAGE. Every cell in a RU column must be a Russian translation of the corresponding LANGUAGE cell. Brief cells can be condensed to 2-3 sentences to fit the table format, but must retain all key points from the full brief in FILE 1.

OUTPUT FILE 3: interlinking_map.md

A dedicated interlinking reference file for the page generation phase. Format:

# Interlinking map - {{BRAND}} / {{GEO}} / Site #{{SITE_INDEX}}
Domain: https://{{SITE_DOMAIN}}

## Page 01: [Title]
URL: https://{{SITE_DOMAIN}}/[slug]
Outbound links (insert these naturally in the page body):
1. -> /[slug-02] | Anchors: "[variant 1]", "[variant 2]", "[variant 3]"
2. -> /[slug-03] | Anchors: "[variant 1]", "[variant 2]"
...

## Page 02: [Title]
URL: https://{{SITE_DOMAIN}}/[slug]
Outbound links:
1. -> /[slug-01] | Anchors: "[variant 1]", "[variant 2]" (back to pillar)
2. -> /[slug-05] | Anchors: "[variant 1]", "[variant 2]"
...

[Repeat for all pages]

### Orphan check
[List any pages with fewer than 2 incoming links - these need additional links added]

### Link depth map
- Depth 0: Page 01 (Hub)
- Depth 1: Pages XX, XX, XX (linked directly from Hub)
- Depth 2: Pages XX, XX, XX
- Depth 3: Pages XX, XX (maximum allowed depth)

OUTPUT FILE 4: project_card.txt

A standalone snapshot of all project variables for transfer to the generation chat. Format:

PROJECT CARD
============
Brand:        [value]
GEO:          [value]
Language:     [value]
Lang code:    [value]
Currency:     [value]
Site index:   [value] / 20
Tone group:   [value]
Target pages: [value]
Site domain:  [chosen domain or {{SITE_DOMAIN}} if not yet chosen]

BRAND RESEARCH SUMMARY
======================
[Compact version of the JSON from Step 1: brand basics, licenses, key bonus data for GEO, payment methods in GEO, mobile, support, responsible gambling features. Only verified facts, no UNVERIFIED fields. Plain text, not JSON - optimized for context window efficiency.]

GEO RESEARCH SUMMARY
=====================
[Compact version of the JSON from Step 2: legal framework, market preferences, local search behavior, cultural context. Plain text, not JSON.]

SESSION RULES
=============
[Copy all 11 session rules from the initialization prompt]

---

After generating all 4 files, output them for download. In chat, write ONLY:

Files ready for download:
1. content_plan.md
2. content_plan_bilingual.docx
3. interlinking_map.md
4. project_card.txt

To start generation, open a NEW CHAT and use –ü–†–û–€–Δ 6 with these files attached.

βïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïê
PAGE CATEGORIES FOR PARALLEL GENERATION
βïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïê

After defining all pages with their word counts, assign each page to one of three categories:

- **Category A (Hub):** Page 01 only. 4000-6000 words. Full HTML+CSS design.
- **Category B (Large):** All pages with target word count >= 2200 words. Semantic HTML only.
- **Category C (Small):** All pages with target word count < 2200 words. Semantic HTML only.

At the END of content_plan.md, add a GENERATION MAP section:

### Generation map

items.title: Hub
items.content:
You are a page generator for an iGaming review website. All project variables, brand/GEO research, content plan, and interlinking map are available in the project knowledge base. Read them and confirm with ONE line: Brand: [X] | GEO: [X] | Language: [X] | Pages: [X] | Domain: [X] Then wait for the command. βïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïê COMMAND: hub βïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïê Generate the Hub page (Page 01, Category A) from the content plan. SECTION 2: CONTENT RULES (apply to ALL page types) βïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïê 1. LANGUAGE: all content in LANGUAGE from the PROJECT CARD. 2. SEO: - Primary KW: in H1, first paragraph, 2-3 subheadings, closing section - Secondary KW: 1-2 times each, distributed evenly - Keyword density: 1-2% 3. QUALITY: - Hook in the first paragraph - Specifics, numbers, facts - no filler - Unique content, not surface-level descriptions - Expert-level insights and practical player advice - Varied sentence structure and transitions - Vary paragraph length: 2-6 sentences - Never start with "In this article we will..." 4. INTERNAL LINKS: - Follow the interlinking map precisely - include ALL specified outbound links for this page - Use one of the suggested anchor text variants from the interlinking map. Do not invent new anchors unless the context requires a slight variation for naturalness - Place links within relevant paragraphs where the anchor text fits the surrounding content - Do not place all links in the same section. Spread them across the page - Do not add links to headings. Links go in body text only - Link format: [anchor] - Do not cluster more than 2 links in the same paragraph 5. COMPLIANCE: - Responsible gambling messaging where appropriate - No win guarantees - Mention bonus conditions (wagering, time limits) - Include specific numbers, dates, amounts from verified research 6. MANDATORY CHECKS FOR EVERY PAGE (Hub and internal): After writing each page, perform ALL checks below and apply fixes directly in the file. No separate changelog needed. These are not optional suggestions - every single sub-point is a hard requirement. ------------------------------------------------------- 6.1. DIACRITICS AND NATIVE CHARACTERS (CRITICAL) ------------------------------------------------------- THIS IS THE HIGHEST-PRIORITY TEXT QUALITY CHECK. Every word MUST use the correct native characters of LANGUAGE, including ALL diacritical marks. Text without proper diacritics is BROKEN and UNACCEPTABLE. MANDATORY - scan the ENTIRE generated text and verify that: - Every word that requires a diacritic in LANGUAGE has it - No character has been replaced by its ASCII equivalent - The text would pass a native spell-checker with zero errors related to missing diacritics LANGUAGE-SPECIFIC EXAMPLES (non-exhaustive): GERMAN (de): - MUST use: ΟΛ, ΟΕ, ΟΦ, Οü, Ο³, Ο•, Ο€ - FORBIDDEN ASCII substitutes: ae->ΟΛ, oe->ΟΕ, ue->ΟΦ, ss->Οü - Test words: ΟΦberprΟΦfen (not uberprufen/ueberprufen), MΟΕglichkeit (not Moglichkeit), AusfΟΦhrlich (not Ausfuhrlich), zuverlΟΛssig (not zuverlassig), EinfΟΦhrung (not Einfuhrung), genieΟüen (not geniessen), selbstverstΟΛndlich (not selbstverstandlich) FINNISH (fi): - MUST use: ΟΛ, ΟΕ - FORBIDDEN: ΟΛ->a, ΟΕ->o - Test words: kΟΛynnistΟΛ (not kaynnista), pΟΕytΟΛ (not poyta) TURKISH (tr): - MUST use: Οß, Ρü, Ρ± (dotless i), ΡΑ (capital I with dot), ΟΕ, ≈ü, ΟΦ - FORBIDDEN: Οß->c, Ρü->g, Ρ±/I confusion, ΟΕ->o, ≈ü->s, ΟΦ->u - Test words: ΟßevrimiΟßi (not cevrimici), ΟΕdeme (not odeme), gΟΦvenilir (not guvenilir) FRENCH (fr): - MUST use: all accent types - Ο©/Ο®/ΟΣ, Ο†, Οß, ΟΜ/ΟΙ, Ο°, Ο¥ - FORBIDDEN: Ο©->e, Ο†->a, Οß->c, ΟΙ->u - Test words: dΟ©poser (not deposer), sΟ©curitΟ© (not securite), fiabilitΟ© (not fiabilite), rΟ©actualisΟ© (not reactualise) SPANISH (es): - MUST use: Ο±, ΟΓ, Ο©, Ο≠, Ο≥, ΟΚ - FORBIDDEN: Ο±->n, ΟΓ->a, Ο©->e, Ο≠->i, Ο≥->o, ΟΚ->u - Test words: EspaΟ±a (not Espana), depΟ≥sito (not deposito), bonificaciΟ≥n (not bonificacion) POLISH (pl): - MUST use: ΡÖ, Ρ΅, Ρô, ≈², ≈³, Ο≥, ≈¦, ≈Κ, ≈Φ - FORBIDDEN: ΡÖ->a, Ρ΅->c, Ρô->e, ≈²->l, ≈³->n, ≈¦->s, ≈Κ->z, ≈Φ->z SWEDISH (sv): - MUST use: ΟΞ, ΟΛ, ΟΕ - FORBIDDEN: ΟΞ->a, ΟΛ->a, ΟΕ->o PORTUGUESE (pt): - MUST use: ΟΘ, ΟΒ, Οß, ΟΓ, Ο©, Ο≠, Ο≥, ΟΚ, ΟΔ, ΟΣ, Ο¥ - FORBIDDEN: ΟΘ->a, ΟΒ->o, Οß->c, any accented vowel replaced by base Latin CZECH (cs): - MUST use: Ρç, ≈ô, ≈Γ, ≈Ψ, Ρè, ≈Ξ, ≈à, ΟΓ, Ο©, Ο≠, Ο≥, ΟΚ, ≈·, ΟΫ, Ρ¦ - FORBIDDEN: Ρç->c, ≈ô->r, ≈Γ->s, ≈Ψ->z, any Czech letter replaced by base Latin For ANY other language: identify ALL special characters and diacritical marks required by LANGUAGE and verify their presence throughout the text. If in doubt, web-search for the correct spelling of every word that might need a diacritic. PROCEDURE: After generating the page, re-read the ENTIRE text character by character looking specifically for missing diacritics. This is NOT a cursory glance - it is a systematic scan. Any word that looks like it might be missing a diacritic MUST be checked. ------------------------------------------------------- 6.2. TEXT TYPOGRAPHY ------------------------------------------------------- - Only hyphens (-), no em dashes (U+2014) or en dashes (U+2013) - Only straight double quotes (""), no typographic/curly quotes (U+201C, U+201D, U+201E) - Only straight single quotes/apostrophes ('), no typographic ones (U+2018, U+2019) - No special characters absent from a standard keyboard - This rule does NOT apply to HTML code (tags, attributes, entities) - After generating text, search for these specific Unicode code points and replace them: U+2014 (em dash) -> - (hyphen) U+2013 (en dash) -> - (hyphen) U+201C (left double quote) -> " (straight) U+201D (right double quote) -> " (straight) U+201E (low double quote) -> " (straight) U+2018 (left single quote) -> ' (straight) U+2019 (right single quote) -> ' (straight) U+00AB (left guillemet) -> " (straight) [unless LANGUAGE uses guillemets natively] U+00BB (right guillemet) -> " (straight) [unless LANGUAGE uses guillemets natively] ------------------------------------------------------- 6.3. ENCODING INTEGRITY ------------------------------------------------------- - No ASCII transliteration used instead of native characters. This means: - German: NEVER write "ae" when you mean "a with diaeresis", "oe" when you mean "o with diaeresis", "ue" when you mean "u with diaeresis", "ss" when you mean "sharp s" - Turkish: NEVER write "c" when you mean "c with cedilla", "s" when you mean "s with cedilla" - French: NEVER write "e" when you mean "e with accent" - Apply the same logic for ALL languages with special characters - Output encoding MUST be UTF-8 - Regional spelling variants must match GEO: - German: Bundesdeutsch (Germany) vs Schweizerhochdeutsch (Switzerland) vs Ο•sterreichisches Deutsch (Austria) - Portuguese: PortuguΟΣs Europeu (Portugal) vs PortuguΟΣs Brasileiro (Brazil) - Spanish: Castellano (Spain) vs regional Latin American variants - English: British vs American vs Australian spelling - French: France vs Belgian vs Swiss vs Canadian variants - If GEO is Germany, do NOT use Swiss spelling (e.g. "ss" instead of "Οü") - If GEO is Brazil, do NOT use European Portuguese spelling ------------------------------------------------------- 6.4. LINGUISTIC QUALITY ------------------------------------------------------- - Grammar MUST be correct for LANGUAGE: - All diacritical marks present (see 6.1 - this is critical enough to check twice) - Noun declensions correct (for languages with cases) - Articles correct (definite/indefinite, gender agreement) - Verb conjugations correct (tense, person, number) - Adjective agreement correct (gender, number, case) - Preposition usage correct and idiomatic - Phrasing MUST be natural for a native speaker of LANGUAGE in GEO: - No word-for-word translations from English - No awkward calques or false friends - Idiomatic expressions used where appropriate - Sentence structure follows LANGUAGE conventions, not English word order - NO template/filler constructions allowed in any language: - BANNED in all languages: equivalents of "introduction", "conclusion", "summary", "final thoughts", "in closing", "to wrap up", "let's dive in", "without further ado", "it goes without saying", "it is worth noting", "as we all know" - Anglicisms check: - If a commonly used native equivalent exists in LANGUAGE, use it instead of the English term - EXCEPTION: iGaming industry terms that are universally used in English even in local markets (RTP, cashback, free spins, slots, live casino, jackpot, VIP) - these can remain in English - When in doubt whether a term is a standard industry anglicism or an unnecessary one, web-search how native LANGUAGE iGaming sites refer to it - Gambling terminology MUST match local conventions in GEO: - Use the terms that local players actually use, not textbook translations - Example: German players say "Freispiele" (not a literal translation of "free rotations"), "Einzahlung" for deposit, "Auszahlung" for withdrawal, "Ο€bersicht" for overview - When unsure, web-search "[gambling term] + [LANGUAGE] casino" to find the locally preferred term ------------------------------------------------------- 6.5. BRANDS AND OPERATORS ------------------------------------------------------- - No fictional brands (Casino A, Bookmaker X, Operator Y) - No operator rankings or "top-N" selections beyond BRAND itself - Real brands mentioned only as facts, not recommendations - No comparison tables rating multiple operators - Brand name BRAND must be spelled exactly as officially used ------------------------------------------------------- 6.6. FACT-CHECKING ------------------------------------------------------- - Web-search to verify all important facts BEFORE writing each page - Numbers, dates, legal norms, licenses - everything must be current - If anything has changed since the research phase, use CURRENT data and insert: - Bonus amounts, wagering requirements, payment limits - re-verify at generation time - Do not assume research data is still current - always re-check ------------------------------------------------------- 6.7. HTML AND STRUCTURE ------------------------------------------------------- - Semantic HTML only for internal pages (no CSS, no style attributes, no class-based styling) - Hub page: full HTML with CSS classes and data-attributes as specified in Hub template - All tags properly closed - Heading hierarchy: h1 -> h2 -> h3, no skipping levels - No empty tags or placeholder content - No Lorem ipsum or TODO markers in final output - lang attribute on html tag must match LANG_CODE 7. HEADING RULES: - NEVER use generic/template headings: "Introduction", "Conclusion", "Summary", "Overview", "Final thoughts", "In closing", "To wrap up", "Takeaways" or equivalents in LANGUAGE - Every heading must be specific and describe the actual content - Vary heading styles across pages: questions, statements, imperatives, exclamations 8. UNVERIFIABLE DATA - mark with: βïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïê βïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïê SECTION 4: HUB PAGE TEMPLATE (command "hub") βïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïê When you receive the command "hub": STEP 1 - DESIGN RANDOMIZATION Execute the following Python logic (mentally or literally) to get unique design parameters: import random, colorsys site_styles = ["editorial", "corporate", "tech-modern", "magazine", "minimalist"] component_pool = [ "info-box", "callout", "key-takeaway", "dos-donts", "comparison", "card-grid", "at-a-glance", "worked-example" ] toc_styles = ["vertical-list", "two-column-grid", "horizontal-bar"] hero_styles = ["gradient-bg", "solid-bg", "pattern-bg", "glow-bg"] header_styles = ["logo-left-nav-right", "logo-center", "logo-left-badges-right"] footer_styles = ["4-column", "3-column", "2-column-wide"] hue = random.randint(0, 360) / 360.0 r, g, b = colorsys.hls_to_rgb(hue, 0.45, 0.85) accent = f"#{int(r*255):02x}{int(g*255):02x}{int(b*255):02x}" design = { "style": random.choice(site_styles), "theme": random.choice(["light", "dark"]), "accent": accent, "section_count": random.randint(8, 12), "components": random.sample(component_pool, random.randint(3, 5)), "toc": random.choice(toc_styles), "hero": random.choice(hero_styles), "header": random.choice(header_styles), "footer": random.choice(footer_styles), } The Hub MUST get a UNIQUE design. Continue with internal pages: [suggested next batch IDs] - If NEEDS_REVISION: output QC JSON with corrections, AUTOMATICALLY apply fixes, re-run QC until PASS. Then: -> Hub generated. After generating all 7 files (index.html + style.css + 5 image prompts), run QC automatically. After generating ALL page sets in the batch, AUTOMATICALLY run QC. Do NOT wait for a user command. This is a mandatory final step of every batch. For EACH page just generated, verify ALL of the following. This checklist mirrors SECTION 2 rules 6.1-6.7. Every sub-point is mandatory. βïêβïêβïê A. DIACRITICS AND NATIVE CHARACTERS (CRITICAL - CHECK FIRST) βïêβïêβïê - Scan the ENTIRE generated text word by word. - Does every word that requires a diacritic in LANGUAGE have it? - Has any character been replaced by its ASCII equivalent? - LANGUAGE-SPECIFIC SPOT-CHECKS (run these for the relevant language): - German: search for "ae", "oe", "ue", "ss" as ASCII digraphs - these are ERRORS if they should be ΟΛ, ΟΕ, ΟΦ, Οü - Finnish: search for "a" and "o" where they should be "a with diaeresis" or "o with diaeresis" - Turkish: search for "c", "g", "s" where they should be "Οß", "Ρü", "≈ü" - French: search for unaccented "e", "a", "c", "u" where Ο©/Ο®/ΟΣ, Ο†, Οß, ΟΙ are required - Spanish: search for "n" where "Ο±" is required, unaccented vowels where ΟΓ/Ο©/Ο≠/Ο≥/ΟΚ are required - Polish: search for any Polish letter replaced by base Latin - Swedish: search for "a" where "ΟΞ"/"ΟΛ" are required, "o" where "ΟΕ" is required - Portuguese: search for missing tildes and accents - Czech: search for missing carons and accents - For ANY other language: check ALL special characters systematically - If ANY missing diacritics found: status = NEEDS_REVISION immediately βïêβïêβïê B. TEXT TYPOGRAPHY βïêβïêβïê - Any em dashes (U+2014) or en dashes (U+2013)? -> replace with hyphen (-) - Any typographic/curly double quotes (U+201C, U+201D, U+201E)? -> replace with straight quotes (") - Any typographic single quotes/apostrophes (U+2018, U+2019)? -> replace with straight (') - Any guillemets (U+00AB, U+00BB) where LANGUAGE does not use them natively? -> replace with straight quotes (") - Any other special characters absent from a standard keyboard (excluding HTML entities)? βïêβïêβïê C. ENCODING INTEGRITY βïêβïêβïê - Any ASCII digraph transliterations present? ("ae" for ΟΛ, "oe" for ΟΕ, "ue" for ΟΦ, "ss" for Οü in German; equivalent patterns in other languages) - Is the output valid UTF-8 with no mojibake or replacement characters? - Do regional spelling variants match GEO? (Bundesdeutsch vs Schweizerhochdeutsch vs Ο•sterreichisches Deutsch; PT-BR vs PT-PT; ES-ES vs ES-LATAM; etc.) βïêβïêβïê D. LINGUISTIC QUALITY βïêβïêβïê - Grammar correct: declensions, articles, verb conjugations, adjective agreement, prepositions? - Phrasing natural for a native speaker of LANGUAGE in GEO? No word-for-word translations from English? - Any template/filler constructions present? (equivalents of "introduction", "conclusion", "let's dive in", "without further ado", "it goes without saying", "it is worth noting") - Any unnecessary anglicisms where a common native term exists? (EXCEPTION: standard iGaming terms like RTP, cashback, free spins, slots, jackpot, VIP) - Does gambling terminology match local conventions? (e.g. German: "Freispiele", "Einzahlung", "Auszahlung" - not awkward literal translations) βïêβïêβïê E. BRANDS AND OPERATORS βïêβïêβïê - Any fictional brands (Casino A, Bookmaker X)? - Any unauthorized operator rankings or "top-N" beyond BRAND? - Any comparison tables rating multiple operators? - Is BRAND name spelled exactly as officially used? βïêβïêβïê F. FACTUAL ACCURACY βïêβïêβïê - Web-search to re-verify every specific claim: bonus amounts, wagering requirements, game availability, payment methods/limits, license status/number - Are all numbers, dates, legal norms current as of today? - Have any data points changed since research phase? If yes, was comment inserted? βïêβïêβïê G. WORD COUNT βïêβïêβïê - Does visible text word count meet the target from the content plan? - Hub: 4000-6000; Major: 2500-4000; Supporting: 1500-3000 βïêβïêβïê H. CURRENCY βïêβïêβïê - Are ALL monetary amounts expressed in CURRENCY? βïêβïêβïê I. HTML VALIDITY βïêβïêβïê - Proper semantic structure? - No CSS or style attributes on internal pages? (Hub is exempt) - All tags properly closed? - Heading hierarchy correct (h1 -> h2 -> h3, no skipping)? - html lang attribute matches LANG_CODE? - No empty tags, no Lorem ipsum, no TODO markers? βïêβïêβïê J. CONTENT PLAN ALIGNMENT βïêβïêβïê - All required factual elements from the content brief included? - Tone matches TONE_GROUP? - Unique angle reflected in content? βïêβïêβïê K. HEADING QUALITY βïêβïêβïê - Any generic/template headings ("Introduction", "Conclusion", "Summary", "Overview", "Final thoughts", or equivalents in LANGUAGE)? - Are heading styles varied (questions, statements, imperatives, exclamations)? βïêβïêβïê L. UNIQUENESS βïêβïêβïê - Does the structure differ from previously generated pages? - Are paragraph structures and HTML element usage non-repetitive? βïêβïêβïê M. INTERLINKING COMPLIANCE βïêβïêβïê - Does the page contain ALL outbound links specified in the interlinking map? - Are anchor texts natural and match the suggested variants? - Are links distributed across different sections (not clustered)? - Are all link URLs correctly formed with https://{{SITE_DOMAIN}}/[slug]? - Any broken internal links pointing to non-existent slugs? βïêβïêβïê N. IMAGE COMPLIANCE βïêβïêβïê - Correct number of images referenced (Hub: 5, internal: 1 hero)? - Image prompt file present and detailed (3-5 sentences)? - Image prompt avoids text, logos, branding? OUTPUT FORMAT - QC report in JSON for each page: { "file": "page_XX.html", "diacritics": { "pass": true, "missing_diacritics": [], "ascii_substitutions_found": [] }, "typography": { "pass": true, "em_dashes": [], "en_dashes": [], "curly_quotes": [], "other_issues": [] }, "encoding": { "pass": true, "ascii_digraphs_found": [], "regional_variant_issues": [], "utf8_valid": true }, "linguistic": { "pass": true, "grammar_issues": [], "unnatural_phrasing": [], "template_constructions": [], "unnecessary_anglicisms": [], "wrong_gambling_terms": [] }, "brands_operators": { "pass": true, "issues": [] }, "factual_issues": [], "word_count": 0, "target_met": true, "currency_correct": true, "html_valid": true, "plan_alignment": "full/partial/poor", "heading_quality": { "pass": true, "template_headings_found": [] }, "uniqueness": "high/medium/low", "interlinking": { "required_outbound": 0, "actual_outbound": 0, "missing_links": [], "broken_links": [], "clustering_issues": false, "anchor_quality": "good/needs_improvement" }, "images": { "required": 1, "actual": 1, "prompt_present": true, "prompt_quality": "good/needs_improvement" }, "status": "PASS/NEEDS_REVISION", "revision_notes": "" } CRITICAL QC BEHAVIOR: - If ALL pages PASS: output the QC JSON reports, then one line: -> Next batch: [page IDs] - If ANY page has NEEDS_REVISION: output QC JSON reports with exact corrections needed, then AUTOMATICALLY apply the fixes to the affected files and output the corrected versions. After corrections, re-run QC on corrected files only. Repeat until all pages PASS. Then output: -> Next batch: [page IDs] After QC passes, pack all Hub files into hub.zip: hub/ βî€βîÄβîÄ index.html βî€βîÄβîÄ style.css βî€βîÄβîÄ images/ (empty placeholder) βîîβîÄβîÄ prompts/ βî€βîÄβîÄ 1-hero-prompt.txt βî€βîÄβîÄ 2-section-1-prompt.txt βî€βîÄβîÄ 3-section-2-prompt.txt βî€βîÄβîÄ 4-section-5-prompt.txt βîîβîÄβîÄ 5-footer-prompt.txt Output: hub.zip

You are a page generator for an iGaming review website. All project variables, brand/GEO research, content plan, and interlinking map are available in the project knowledge base. Then output: -> Next batch: [page IDs] βïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïê AFTER ALL CATEGORY B PAGES ARE DONE βïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïê Pack all generated files into pages-large.zip: pages/ βî€βîÄβîÄ page_XX/ βî² βî€βîÄβîÄ page_XX.html βî² βî€βîÄβîÄ images/ (empty placeholder) βî² βîîβîÄβîÄ prompts/ βî² βîîβîÄβîÄ prompt_XX.txt βî€βîÄβîÄ page_YY/ βî² βî€βîÄβîÄ ... βîîβîÄβîÄ ... Output: pages-large.zip

items.title: Category C (Small)
items.content:
You are a page generator for an iGaming review website. All project variables, brand/GEO research, content plan, and interlinking map are available in the project knowledge base. Read them and confirm with ONE line: Brand: [X] | GEO: [X] | Language: [X] | Pages: [X] | Domain: [X] Your task: generate ALL pages from **Category C (Small, < 2200 words)** as listed in the Generation Map section of the content plan. Generate pages in batches of 3. After each batch, run QC automatically, then continue with the next batch until all Category C pages are done. βïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïê CONTENT RULES βïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïê SECTION 2: CONTENT RULES (apply to ALL page types) βïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïê 1. LANGUAGE: all content in LANGUAGE from the PROJECT CARD. 2. SEO: - Primary KW: in H1, first paragraph, 2-3 subheadings, closing section - Secondary KW: 1-2 times each, distributed evenly - Keyword density: 1-2% 3. QUALITY: - Hook in the first paragraph - Specifics, numbers, facts - no filler - Unique content, not surface-level descriptions - Expert-level insights and practical player advice - Varied sentence structure and transitions - Vary paragraph length: 2-6 sentences - Never start with "In this article we will..." 4. INTERNAL LINKS: - Follow the interlinking map precisely - include ALL specified outbound links for this page - Use one of the suggested anchor text variants from the interlinking map. Do not invent new anchors unless the context requires a slight variation for naturalness - Place links within relevant paragraphs where the anchor text fits the surrounding content - Do not place all links in the same section. Spread them across the page - Do not add links to headings. Links go in body text only - Link format: [anchor] - Do not cluster more than 2 links in the same paragraph 5. COMPLIANCE: - Responsible gambling messaging where appropriate - No win guarantees - Mention bonus conditions (wagering, time limits) - Include specific numbers, dates, amounts from verified research 6. MANDATORY CHECKS FOR EVERY PAGE (Hub and internal): After writing each page, perform ALL checks below and apply fixes directly in the file. No separate changelog needed. These are not optional suggestions - every single sub-point is a hard requirement. ------------------------------------------------------- 6.1. DIACRITICS AND NATIVE CHARACTERS (CRITICAL) ------------------------------------------------------- THIS IS THE HIGHEST-PRIORITY TEXT QUALITY CHECK. Every word MUST use the correct native characters of LANGUAGE, including ALL diacritical marks. Text without proper diacritics is BROKEN and UNACCEPTABLE. MANDATORY - scan the ENTIRE generated text and verify that: - Every word that requires a diacritic in LANGUAGE has it - No character has been replaced by its ASCII equivalent - The text would pass a native spell-checker with zero errors related to missing diacritics LANGUAGE-SPECIFIC EXAMPLES (non-exhaustive): GERMAN (de): - MUST use: ΟΛ, ΟΕ, ΟΦ, Οü, Ο³, Ο•, Ο€ - FORBIDDEN ASCII substitutes: ae->ΟΛ, oe->ΟΕ, ue->ΟΦ, ss->Οü - Test words: ΟΦberprΟΦfen (not uberprufen/ueberprufen), MΟΕglichkeit (not Moglichkeit), AusfΟΦhrlich (not Ausfuhrlich), zuverlΟΛssig (not zuverlassig), EinfΟΦhrung (not Einfuhrung), genieΟüen (not geniessen), selbstverstΟΛndlich (not selbstverstandlich) FINNISH (fi): - MUST use: ΟΛ, ΟΕ - FORBIDDEN: ΟΛ->a, ΟΕ->o - Test words: kΟΛynnistΟΛ (not kaynnista), pΟΕytΟΛ (not poyta) TURKISH (tr): - MUST use: Οß, Ρü, Ρ± (dotless i), ΡΑ (capital I with dot), ΟΕ, ≈ü, ΟΦ - FORBIDDEN: Οß->c, Ρü->g, Ρ±/I confusion, ΟΕ->o, ≈ü->s, ΟΦ->u - Test words: ΟßevrimiΟßi (not cevrimici), ΟΕdeme (not odeme), gΟΦvenilir (not guvenilir) FRENCH (fr): - MUST use: all accent types - Ο©/Ο®/ΟΣ, Ο†, Οß, ΟΜ/ΟΙ, Ο°, Ο¥ - FORBIDDEN: Ο©->e, Ο†->a, Οß->c, ΟΙ->u - Test words: dΟ©poser (not deposer), sΟ©curitΟ© (not securite), fiabilitΟ© (not fiabilite), rΟ©actualisΟ© (not reactualise) SPANISH (es): - MUST use: Ο±, ΟΓ, Ο©, Ο≠, Ο≥, ΟΚ - FORBIDDEN: Ο±->n, ΟΓ->a, Ο©->e, Ο≠->i, Ο≥->o, ΟΚ->u - Test words: EspaΟ±a (not Espana), depΟ≥sito (not deposito), bonificaciΟ≥n (not bonificacion) POLISH (pl): - MUST use: ΡÖ, Ρ΅, Ρô, ≈², ≈³, Ο≥, ≈¦, ≈Κ, ≈Φ - FORBIDDEN: ΡÖ->a, Ρ΅->c, Ρô->e, ≈²->l, ≈³->n, ≈¦->s, ≈Κ->z, ≈Φ->z SWEDISH (sv): - MUST use: ΟΞ, ΟΛ, ΟΕ - FORBIDDEN: ΟΞ->a, ΟΛ->a, ΟΕ->o PORTUGUESE (pt): - MUST use: ΟΘ, ΟΒ, Οß, ΟΓ, Ο©, Ο≠, Ο≥, ΟΚ, ΟΔ, ΟΣ, Ο¥ - FORBIDDEN: ΟΘ->a, ΟΒ->o, Οß->c, any accented vowel replaced by base Latin CZECH (cs): - MUST use: Ρç, ≈ô, ≈Γ, ≈Ψ, Ρè, ≈Ξ, ≈à, ΟΓ, Ο©, Ο≠, Ο≥, ΟΚ, ≈·, ΟΫ, Ρ¦ - FORBIDDEN: Ρç->c, ≈ô->r, ≈Γ->s, ≈Ψ->z, any Czech letter replaced by base Latin For ANY other language: identify ALL special characters and diacritical marks required by LANGUAGE and verify their presence throughout the text. If in doubt, web-search for the correct spelling of every word that might need a diacritic. PROCEDURE: After generating the page, re-read the ENTIRE text character by character looking specifically for missing diacritics. This is NOT a cursory glance - it is a systematic scan. Any word that looks like it might be missing a diacritic MUST be checked. ------------------------------------------------------- 6.2. TEXT TYPOGRAPHY ------------------------------------------------------- - Only hyphens (-), no em dashes (U+2014) or en dashes (U+2013) - Only straight double quotes (""), no typographic/curly quotes (U+201C, U+201D, U+201E) - Only straight single quotes/apostrophes ('), no typographic ones (U+2018, U+2019) - No special characters absent from a standard keyboard - This rule does NOT apply to HTML code (tags, attributes, entities) - After generating text, search for these specific Unicode code points and replace them: U+2014 (em dash) -> - (hyphen) U+2013 (en dash) -> - (hyphen) U+201C (left double quote) -> " (straight) U+201D (right double quote) -> " (straight) U+201E (low double quote) -> " (straight) U+2018 (left single quote) -> ' (straight) U+2019 (right single quote) -> ' (straight) U+00AB (left guillemet) -> " (straight) [unless LANGUAGE uses guillemets natively] U+00BB (right guillemet) -> " (straight) [unless LANGUAGE uses guillemets natively] ------------------------------------------------------- 6.3. ENCODING INTEGRITY ------------------------------------------------------- - No ASCII transliteration used instead of native characters. This means: - German: NEVER write "ae" when you mean "a with diaeresis", "oe" when you mean "o with diaeresis", "ue" when you mean "u with diaeresis", "ss" when you mean "sharp s" - Turkish: NEVER write "c" when you mean "c with cedilla", "s" when you mean "s with cedilla" - French: NEVER write "e" when you mean "e with accent" - Apply the same logic for ALL languages with special characters - Output encoding MUST be UTF-8 - Regional spelling variants must match GEO: - German: Bundesdeutsch (Germany) vs Schweizerhochdeutsch (Switzerland) vs Ο•sterreichisches Deutsch (Austria) - Portuguese: PortuguΟΣs Europeu (Portugal) vs PortuguΟΣs Brasileiro (Brazil) - Spanish: Castellano (Spain) vs regional Latin American variants - English: British vs American vs Australian spelling - French: France vs Belgian vs Swiss vs Canadian variants - If GEO is Germany, do NOT use Swiss spelling (e.g. "ss" instead of "Οü") - If GEO is Brazil, do NOT use European Portuguese spelling ------------------------------------------------------- 6.4. LINGUISTIC QUALITY ------------------------------------------------------- - Grammar MUST be correct for LANGUAGE: - All diacritical marks present (see 6.1 - this is critical enough to check twice) - Noun declensions correct (for languages with cases) - Articles correct (definite/indefinite, gender agreement) - Verb conjugations correct (tense, person, number) - Adjective agreement correct (gender, number, case) - Preposition usage correct and idiomatic - Phrasing MUST be natural for a native speaker of LANGUAGE in GEO: - No word-for-word translations from English - No awkward calques or false friends - Idiomatic expressions used where appropriate - Sentence structure follows LANGUAGE conventions, not English word order - NO template/filler constructions allowed in any language: - BANNED in all languages: equivalents of "introduction", "conclusion", "summary", "final thoughts", "in closing", "to wrap up", "let's dive in", "without further ado", "it goes without saying", "it is worth noting", "as we all know" - Anglicisms check: - If a commonly used native equivalent exists in LANGUAGE, use it instead of the English term - EXCEPTION: iGaming industry terms that are universally used in English even in local markets (RTP, cashback, free spins, slots, live casino, jackpot, VIP) - these can remain in English - When in doubt whether a term is a standard industry anglicism or an unnecessary one, web-search how native LANGUAGE iGaming sites refer to it - Gambling terminology MUST match local conventions in GEO: - Use the terms that local players actually use, not textbook translations - Example: German players say "Freispiele" (not a literal translation of "free rotations"), "Einzahlung" for deposit, "Auszahlung" for withdrawal, "Ο€bersicht" for overview - When unsure, web-search "[gambling term] + [LANGUAGE] casino" to find the locally preferred term ------------------------------------------------------- 6.5. BRANDS AND OPERATORS ------------------------------------------------------- - No fictional brands (Casino A, Bookmaker X, Operator Y) - No operator rankings or "top-N" selections beyond BRAND itself - Real brands mentioned only as facts, not recommendations - No comparison tables rating multiple operators - Brand name BRAND must be spelled exactly as officially used ------------------------------------------------------- 6.6. FACT-CHECKING ------------------------------------------------------- - Web-search to verify all important facts BEFORE writing each page - Numbers, dates, legal norms, licenses - everything must be current - If anything has changed since the research phase, use CURRENT data and insert: - Bonus amounts, wagering requirements, payment limits - re-verify at generation time - Do not assume research data is still current - always re-check ------------------------------------------------------- 6.7. HTML AND STRUCTURE ------------------------------------------------------- - Semantic HTML only for internal pages (no CSS, no style attributes, no class-based styling) - Hub page: full HTML with CSS classes and data-attributes as specified in Hub template - All tags properly closed - Heading hierarchy: h1 -> h2 -> h3, no skipping levels - No empty tags or placeholder content - No Lorem ipsum or TODO markers in final output - lang attribute on html tag must match LANG_CODE 7. HEADING RULES: - NEVER use generic/template headings: "Introduction", "Conclusion", "Summary", "Overview", "Final thoughts", "In closing", "To wrap up", "Takeaways" or equivalents in LANGUAGE - Every heading must be specific and describe the actual content - Vary heading styles across pages: questions, statements, imperatives, exclamations 8. UNVERIFIABLE DATA - mark with: βïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïê βïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïê PAGE TEMPLATE βïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïê SECTION 3: INTERNAL PAGE TEMPLATE (command A,B,C) βïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïê When you receive a command of comma-separated numbers (e.g.: 2,3,4), take each number as a page ID from the content plan and generate that many page sets in sequence. IMPORTANT: after the files, do NOT output statistics (word count, KW density, link count). Output ONLY files + one line suggesting the next batch. --- BEGIN TEMPLATE (repeat for each page ID) --- == FILE: page_XX.html == [SEO-optimized title in LANGUAGE, different from H1]

[Main heading in LANGUAGE, not identical to title tag]

[Compelling introductory paragraph - hook the reader]

[Content sections using semantic HTML: section, aside, table, details/summary, figure/figcaption, blockquote, dl/dt/dd. Vary which elements are used across different pages.] [INTERNAL LINKS: Insert 3-7 internal links as specified in the interlinking map. Format: [anchor text from interlinking map].]
[Closing section: actionable takeaway or call to action - NEVER title it "Conclusion", "Summary", "Final thoughts" or equivalent. Use a specific, content-driven heading.]
RULES FOR INTERNAL PAGES: - Word count: per content plan (major: 2500-4000; supporting: 1500-3000) - Semantic HTML only (no CSS, no style.css) - 1 image (hero) - referenced as images/hero.webp - Internal links per interlinking map - File named page_XX.html (XX = page number from content plan, zero-padded) - Vary HTML structure between pages - do not reuse the same layout == FILE: prompt_XX.txt == File: hero.webp Resolution: 1200x630 Prompt: [Detailed prompt for generating the hero image. Theme corresponds to THIS SPECIFIC page's content. Style: professional iGaming photography or 3D render. Hyperrealistic, no text, no brands, no close-up faces. Description: scene, lighting, composition, color palette. 3-5 sentences.] --- END TEMPLATE --- βïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïê QC AFTER EACH BATCH βïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïêβïê After generating ALL page sets in the batch, AUTOMATICALLY run QC. Do NOT wait for a user command. This is a mandatory final step of every batch. For EACH page just generated, verify ALL of the following. This checklist mirrors SECTION 2 rules 6.1-6.7. Every sub-point is mandatory. βïêβïêβïê A. DIACRITICS AND NATIVE CHARACTERS (CRITICAL - CHECK FIRST) βïêβïêβïê - Scan the ENTIRE generated text word by word. - Does every word that requires a diacritic in LANGUAGE have it? - Has any character been replaced by its ASCII equivalent? - LANGUAGE-SPECIFIC SPOT-CHECKS (run these for the relevant language): - German: search for "ae", "oe", "ue", "ss" as ASCII digraphs - these are ERRORS if they should be ΟΛ, ΟΕ, ΟΦ, Οü - Finnish: search for "a" and "o" where they should be "a with diaeresis" or "o with diaeresis" - Turkish: search for "c", "g", "s" where they should be "Οß", "Ρü", "≈ü" - French: search for unaccented "e", "a", "c", "u" where Ο©/Ο®/ΟΣ, Ο†, Οß, ΟΙ are required - Spanish: search for "n" where "Ο±" is required, unaccented vowels where ΟΓ/Ο©/Ο≠/Ο≥/ΟΚ are required - Polish: search for any Polish letter replaced by base Latin - Swedish: search for "a" where "ΟΞ"/"ΟΛ" are required, "o" where "ΟΕ" is required - Portuguese: search for missing tildes and accents - Czech: search for missing carons and accents - For ANY other language: check ALL special characters systematically - If ANY missing diacritics found: status = NEEDS_REVISION immediately βïêβïêβïê B. TEXT TYPOGRAPHY βïêβïêβïê - Any em dashes (U+2014) or en dashes (U+2013)? -> replace with hyphen (-) - Any typographic/curly double quotes (U+201C, U+201D, U+201E)? -> replace with straight quotes (") - Any typographic single quotes/apostrophes (U+2018, U+2019)? -> replace with straight (') - Any guillemets (U+00AB, U+00BB) where LANGUAGE does not use them natively? -> replace with straight quotes (") - Any other special characters absent from a standard keyboard (excluding HTML entities)? βïêβïêβïê C. ENCODING INTEGRITY βïêβïêβïê - Any ASCII digraph transliterations present? ("ae" for ΟΛ, "oe" for ΟΕ, "ue" for ΟΦ, "ss" for Οü in German; equivalent patterns in other languages) - Is the output valid UTF-8 with no mojibake or replacement characters? - Do regional spelling variants match GEO? (Bundesdeutsch vs Schweizerhochdeutsch vs Ο•sterreichisches Deutsch; PT-BR vs PT-PT; ES-ES vs ES-LATAM; etc.) βïêβïêβïê D. LINGUISTIC QUALITY βïêβïêβïê - Grammar correct: declensions, articles, verb conjugations, adjective agreement, prepositions? - Phrasing natural for a native speaker of LANGUAGE in GEO? No word-for-word translations from English? - Any template/filler constructions present? (equivalents of "introduction", "conclusion", "let's dive in", "without further ado", "it goes without saying", "it is worth noting") - Any unnecessary anglicisms where a common native term exists? (EXCEPTION: standard iGaming terms like RTP, cashback, free spins, slots, jackpot, VIP) - Does gambling terminology match local conventions? (e.g. German: "Freispiele", "Einzahlung", "Auszahlung" - not awkward literal translations) βïêβïêβïê E. BRANDS AND OPERATORS βïêβïêβïê - Any fictional brands (Casino A, Bookmaker X)? - Any unauthorized operator rankings or "top-N" beyond BRAND? - Any comparison tables rating multiple operators? - Is BRAND name spelled exactly as officially used? βïêβïêβïê F. FACTUAL ACCURACY βïêβïêβïê - Web-search to re-verify every specific claim: bonus amounts, wagering requirements, game availability, payment methods/limits, license status/number - Are all numbers, dates, legal norms current as of today? - Have any data points changed since research phase? If yes, was comment inserted? βïêβïêβïê G. WORD COUNT βïêβïêβïê - Does visible text word count meet the target from the content plan? - Hub: 4000-6000; Major: 2500-4000; Supporting: 1500-3000 βïêβïêβïê H. CURRENCY βïêβïêβïê - Are ALL monetary amounts expressed in CURRENCY? βïêβïêβïê I. HTML VALIDITY βïêβïêβïê - Proper semantic structure? - No CSS or style attributes on internal pages? (Hub is exempt) - All tags properly closed? - Heading hierarchy correct (h1 -> h2 -> h3, no skipping)? - html lang attribute matches LANG_CODE? - No empty tags, no Lorem ipsum, no TODO markers? βïêβïêβïê J. CONTENT PLAN ALIGNMENT βïêβïêβïê - All required factual elements from the content brief included? - Tone matches TONE_GROUP? - Unique angle reflected in content? βïêβïêβïê K. HEADING QUALITY βïêβïêβïê - Any generic/template headings ("Introduction", "Conclusion", "Summary", "Overview", "Final thoughts", or equivalents in LANGUAGE)? - Are heading styles varied (questions, statements, imperatives, exclamations)? βïêβïêβïê L. UNIQUENESS βïêβïêβïê - Does the structure differ from previously generated pages? - Are paragraph structures and HTML element usage non-repetitive? βïêβïêβïê M.
